---
title: "Theorem 2.9 — Linear projection minimizes MSE"
lean_name: "HansenEconometrics.linearProjectionBeta_minimizes_MSE"
chapter: 2
---

## Textbook statement

Let $Q_{XX}$ be an invertible population second-moment matrix and $Q_{XY}$ the
population cross moment. Define the linear projection coefficient

$$
\beta = Q_{XX}^{-1} Q_{XY}.
$$

For the quadratic population criterion

$$
S(b)=Q_{YY}-2b^\top Q_{XY}+b^\top Q_{XX}b,
$$

the coefficient $\beta$ minimizes $S(b)$ over all coefficient vectors $b$.
The formal theorem assumes the
[Chapter 2 linear-projection hypotheses](../../assumptions/chapter2.qmd):
invertibility, symmetry, and positive semidefiniteness of $Q_{XX}$.

### Proof sketch

Complete the square:

$$
S(b)
=
S(\beta)
+ (b-\beta)^\top Q_{XX}(b-\beta).
$$

Since $Q_{XX}$ is positive semidefinite, the quadratic form is nonnegative.
Therefore $S(\beta)\le S(b)$ for every $b$.

## Lean statement

The theorem lives in
[`HansenEconometrics/Chapter2LinearProjection.lean`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter2LinearProjection.lean#L97):

::: {.lean-hover-panel}
```{=html}
<pre><code><span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="Best-linear-predictor minimization theorem.">theorem linearProjectionBeta_minimizes_MSE</span>
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="Population moment matrix QXX, cross moment QXY, and scalar QYY.">(QXX : Matrix k k ℝ) (QXY : k → ℝ) (QYY : ℝ)</span> [Invertible QXX]
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="Symmetry of the population second-moment matrix.">(hQXXt : QXXᵀ = QXX)</span>
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="Positive semidefiniteness: every quadratic form v'QXXv is nonnegative.">(hQXX_nonneg : ∀ v : k → ℝ, 0 ≤ v ⬝ᵥ (QXX *ᵥ v))</span>
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="An arbitrary competing coefficient vector.">(b : k → ℝ)</span> :
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="The MSE at β = QXX⁻¹QXY is weakly smaller than the MSE at b.">linearProjectionMSE QXX QXY QYY (linearProjectionBeta QXX QXY) ≤
      linearProjectionMSE QXX QXY QYY b</span>
</code></pre>
```
:::

### Reading the statement

| Lean | Math | What it gives us |
|---|---|---|
| `QXX : Matrix k k ℝ` | $Q_{XX}$ | Population second-moment matrix. |
| `QXY : k → ℝ` | $Q_{XY}$ | Population cross moment. |
| `QYY : ℝ` | $Q_{YY}$ | Scalar second moment of $Y$. |
| `[Invertible QXX]` | $Q_{XX}^{-1}$ exists | Needed for `linearProjectionBeta QXX QXY`. |
| `hQXXt : QXXᵀ = QXX` | $Q_{XX}$ symmetric | Used in the quadratic completion. |
| `hQXX_nonneg` | $Q_{XX}$ positive semidefinite | Makes the completed-square term nonnegative. |
| `b : k → ℝ` | competing coefficient | The theorem is pointwise in any alternative $b$. |

## Translation notes

- `linearProjectionBeta QXX QXY` is the formal $\beta = Q_{XX}^{-1}Q_{XY}$.
- `linearProjectionMSE QXX QXY QYY b` is the quadratic criterion $S(b)$.
- The moment-wrapper theorem
  [`linearProjectionBeta_minimizes_MSE_of_moments`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter2LinearProjection.lean#L109)
  has the same conclusion with textbook names `EXX`, `EXY`, and `EY2`.

## Tactic-by-tactic walkthrough

::: {.lean-hover-panel}
```{=html}
<pre><code><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="The minimization theorem: β = QXX⁻¹QXY is no worse than any competing b.">theorem linearProjectionBeta_minimizes_MSE
    (QXX : Matrix k k ℝ) (QXY : k → ℝ) (QYY : ℝ) [Invertible QXX]
    (hQXXt : QXXᵀ = QXX)
    (hQXX_nonneg : ∀ v : k → ℝ, 0 ≤ v ⬝ᵥ (QXX *ᵥ v))
    (b : k → ℝ) :
    linearProjectionMSE QXX QXY QYY (linearProjectionBeta QXX QXY) ≤
      linearProjectionMSE QXX QXY QYY b := by</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Rewrite S(b) as S(β) plus the completed-square quadratic form.">  rw [linearProjectionMSE_eq_at_beta_add_quadratic_form QXX QXY QYY b hQXXt]</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="The added quadratic form is nonnegative by positive semidefiniteness of QXX, so linear arithmetic finishes.">  linarith [hQXX_nonneg (b - linearProjectionBeta QXX QXY)]</span>
</code></pre>
```
:::

The only substantive algebra is the supporting completion theorem
[`linearProjectionMSE_eq_at_beta_add_quadratic_form`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter2LinearProjection.lean#L63),
which expands the criterion, uses the normal equations
`QXX *ᵥ linearProjectionBeta QXX QXY = QXY`, and normalizes the result with
`ring_nf`.

## Downstream uses

- This is the population analogue of the OLS least-squares minimization story
  used in Chapter 3.
- The normal-equation and uniqueness lemmas here are reused by the Chapter 2
  covariance-form slope formula.
